forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,47 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8680'
+title: Spawned claude CLI ignores board cwd's .claude/settings.json permission deny rules
+---
+## What
+
+The kanban-app's in-process AI agent spawns the `claude` CLI with permission enforcement disabled, so a board's local `.claude/settings.json` `permissions.deny` rules (e.g. `deny: ["Bash"]`) are silently ignored. Observed: with `swissarmyhammer-kanban/.claude/settings.json` containing `{"permissions": {"deny": ["Bash"]}}`, the agent still ran the `Bash` tool.
+
+**Root cause** — `crates/claude-agent/src/claude_process.rs`, `CLAUDE_CLI_ARGS` (the const that feeds `ClaudeProcess::build_base_command`) hard-codes:
+
+```rust
+"--dangerously-skip-permissions", // ACP server handles permission checks
+```
+
+`--dangerously-skip-permissions` makes the spawned CLI bypass all of its own permission checks, including filesystem `permissions.deny`/`permissions.allow` resolved from the session's `cwd`. The comment's premise — that the ACP server enforces permissions instead — does not cover the CLI's built-in tools (Bash, Read, Write, etc.): those execute inside the claude process and are never routed through this crate's ACP permission engine (`crates/claude-agent/src/permissions.rs` / `agent_permissions.rs`), which also never loads project `.claude/settings.json` deny/allow lists. Net effect: deny rules in the board cwd are unenforceable.
+
+The session already carries the correct working directory: `SpawnConfig.cwd` (set from the ACP `newSession.cwd`, validated absolute in `session.rs`) and applied via `.current_dir(&config.cwd)` in `execute_spawn`. We just don't translate that directory's settings into anything the CLI honors.
+
+**Approach** — In `crates/claude-agent/src/claude_process.rs`, make the spawned CLI honor the cwd's resolved Claude permission settings:
+
+- [ ] Add a helper that, given `config.cwd`, loads and merges `permissions.deny` and `permissions.allow` from `<cwd>/.claude/settings.json` and `<cwd>/.claude/settings.local.json` (local overrides/extends project). Missing files → empty lists; malformed JSON → log a warning and treat as empty (do not panic the spawn).
+- [ ] When `deny` is non-empty, pass each entry through `--disallowedTools`; when `allow` is non-empty, pass each through `--allowedTools`. Follow the CLI's expected arg shape (verify whether it takes space-separated values, repeated flags, or comma-joined — match what the installed `claude` actually accepts).
+- [ ] Ensure deny is actually enforced. `--dangerously-skip-permissions` overrides `--disallowedTools` in Claude Code, so the two cannot coexist. When the cwd resolves any deny/allow rules, drop `--dangerously-skip-permissions` for that spawn so the CLI enforces them; preserve today's behavior (skip-permissions) only when no project permission rules are present, to avoid regressing the no-settings path.
+- [ ] Keep this scoped to the session `cwd` project settings. User-level (`~/.claude/settings.json`) and enterprise settings are out of scope for this task (note as a follow-up if needed).
+
+Reuse any existing settings struct/parser if the workspace already has one (search `swissarmyhammer-config` / `install/settings.rs` before hand-rolling a serde struct). Keep the new helper small and focused; do not refactor the surrounding spawn pipeline.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] When the session `cwd` has `.claude/settings.json` with `permissions.deny` including `Bash`, the spawn command for that session does NOT include `--dangerously-skip-permissions` and DOES disallow `Bash` (via `--disallowedTools`), so a `Bash` tool call is refused.
+- [ ] `permissions.allow` entries are forwarded via `--allowedTools`.
+- [ ] `.claude/settings.local.json` is merged over/with `.claude/settings.json`.
+- [ ] When the `cwd` has no `.claude` settings (or no `permissions` block), spawn args are unchanged from today (still includes `--dangerously-skip-permissions`) — no regression to the default path.
+- [ ] Missing or malformed settings files do not panic or fail the spawn; they degrade to "no extra permission args".
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] In `crates/claude-agent/src/claude_process.rs` tests, add a unit test that builds a `SpawnConfig` whose `cwd` is a `tempfile::tempdir()` containing `.claude/settings.json` with `{"permissions":{"deny":["Bash"]}}`, builds the command (mirroring the existing `get_args()` inspection tests around the `--mcp-config` cases), and asserts: args contain `--disallowedTools` + `Bash`, and do NOT contain `--dangerously-skip-permissions`.
+- [ ] Unit test: `cwd` with `permissions.allow` → args contain `--allowedTools` + the allowed tool.
+- [ ] Unit test: `cwd` with both `.claude/settings.json` and `.claude/settings.local.json` → merged deny/allow reflected in args.
+- [ ] Regression unit test: `cwd` with no `.claude` dir → args identical to current behavior (includes `--dangerously-skip-permissions`, no `--disallowedTools`/`--allowedTools`).
+- [ ] Unit test: `cwd` with malformed `.claude/settings.json` → spawn-arg build succeeds with no extra permission args (no panic).
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p claude-agent` passes.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing arg-inspection tests first, then implement the settings resolution + arg wiring to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -1,47 +0,0 @@
----
-assignees:
-- claude-code
-position_column: todo
-position_ordinal: '8680'
-title: Spawned claude CLI ignores board cwd's .claude/settings.json permission deny rules
----
-## What
-
-The kanban-app's in-process AI agent spawns the `claude` CLI with permission enforcement disabled, so a board's local `.claude/settings.json` `permissions.deny` rules (e.g. `deny: ["Bash"]`) are silently ignored. Observed: with `swissarmyhammer-kanban/.claude/settings.json` containing `{"permissions": {"deny": ["Bash"]}}`, the agent still ran the `Bash` tool.
-
-**Root cause** — `crates/claude-agent/src/claude_process.rs`, `CLAUDE_CLI_ARGS` (the const that feeds `ClaudeProcess::build_base_command`) hard-codes:
-
-```rust
-"--dangerously-skip-permissions", // ACP server handles permission checks
-```
-
-`--dangerously-skip-permissions` makes the spawned CLI bypass all of its own permission checks, including filesystem `permissions.deny`/`permissions.allow` resolved from the session's `cwd`. The comment's premise — that the ACP server enforces permissions instead — does not cover the CLI's built-in tools (Bash, Read, Write, etc.): those execute inside the claude process and are never routed through this crate's ACP permission engine (`crates/claude-agent/src/permissions.rs` / `agent_permissions.rs`), which also never loads project `.claude/settings.json` deny/allow lists. Net effect: deny rules in the board cwd are unenforceable.
-
-The session already carries the correct working directory: `SpawnConfig.cwd` (set from the ACP `newSession.cwd`, validated absolute in `session.rs`) and applied via `.current_dir(&config.cwd)` in `execute_spawn`. We just don't translate that directory's settings into anything the CLI honors.
-
-**Approach** — In `crates/claude-agent/src/claude_process.rs`, make the spawned CLI honor the cwd's resolved Claude permission settings:
-
-- [ ] Add a helper that, given `config.cwd`, loads and merges `permissions.deny` and `permissions.allow` from `<cwd>/.claude/settings.json` and `<cwd>/.claude/settings.local.json` (local overrides/extends project). Missing files → empty lists; malformed JSON → log a warning and treat as empty (do not panic the spawn).
-- [ ] When `deny` is non-empty, pass each entry through `--disallowedTools`; when `allow` is non-empty, pass each through `--allowedTools`. Follow the CLI's expected arg shape (verify whether it takes space-separated values, repeated flags, or comma-joined — match what the installed `claude` actually accepts).
-- [ ] Ensure deny is actually enforced. `--dangerously-skip-permissions` overrides `--disallowedTools` in Claude Code, so the two cannot coexist. When the cwd resolves any deny/allow rules, drop `--dangerously-skip-permissions` for that spawn so the CLI enforces them; preserve today's behavior (skip-permissions) only when no project permission rules are present, to avoid regressing the no-settings path.
-- [ ] Keep this scoped to the session `cwd` project settings. User-level (`~/.claude/settings.json`) and enterprise settings are out of scope for this task (note as a follow-up if needed).
-
-Reuse any existing settings struct/parser if the workspace already has one (search `swissarmyhammer-config` / `install/settings.rs` before hand-rolling a serde struct). Keep the new helper small and focused; do not refactor the surrounding spawn pipeline.
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] When the session `cwd` has `.claude/settings.json` with `permissions.deny` including `Bash`, the spawn command for that session does NOT include `--dangerously-skip-permissions` and DOES disallow `Bash` (via `--disallowedTools`), so a `Bash` tool call is refused.
-- [ ] `permissions.allow` entries are forwarded via `--allowedTools`.
-- [ ] `.claude/settings.local.json` is merged over/with `.claude/settings.json`.
-- [ ] When the `cwd` has no `.claude` settings (or no `permissions` block), spawn args are unchanged from today (still includes `--dangerously-skip-permissions`) — no regression to the default path.
-- [ ] Missing or malformed settings files do not panic or fail the spawn; they degrade to "no extra permission args".
-
-## Tests
-- [ ] In `crates/claude-agent/src/claude_process.rs` tests, add a unit test that builds a `SpawnConfig` whose `cwd` is a `tempfile::tempdir()` containing `.claude/settings.json` with `{"permissions":{"deny":["Bash"]}}`, builds the command (mirroring the existing `get_args()` inspection tests around the `--mcp-config` cases), and asserts: args contain `--disallowedTools` + `Bash`, and do NOT contain `--dangerously-skip-permissions`.
-- [ ] Unit test: `cwd` with `permissions.allow` → args contain `--allowedTools` + the allowed tool.
-- [ ] Unit test: `cwd` with both `.claude/settings.json` and `.claude/settings.local.json` → merged deny/allow reflected in args.
-- [ ] Regression unit test: `cwd` with no `.claude` dir → args identical to current behavior (includes `--dangerously-skip-permissions`, no `--disallowedTools`/`--allowedTools`).
-- [ ] Unit test: `cwd` with malformed `.claude/settings.json` → spawn-arg build succeeds with no extra permission args (no panic).
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p claude-agent` passes.
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the failing arg-inspection tests first, then implement the settings resolution + arg wiring to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file
